feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Analytics Cloud
feed.subtitle: SAP Analytics Cloud Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Passing parameter from one SAC story to another SAC Story
entry.content:
Hi,

I have a Header and Detail data created in 2 separate stories. The customer wants to have a hyperlink to connect to detail story and show the detail content based on the header strory row where the cursor was.

The detail story model has nearly 1 million row, but if I take all the row level column combination the detail ccould be below a few hundereds.  On the initial build detail could not refresh because too many rows. I cannot apply filter to reduce the volume either.

Any suggestions on filter passing between stories.?

Thank you,

Arthur.

entry.title: sac的年龄计算有问题，目前是出生日期当天年龄就+1，和实际不符合，实际是到达生日后的一天年龄在+1
entry.content: sac的年龄计算有问题，例如陈达新 出生日期是2001年1月31日，截止到2026年1月31日，年龄是24岁，不是现在的25岁，如何处理？

entry.title: Variance Option Missing in Optimized Story Bar Chart
entry.content:
Hi All,

I would like to display a variance on sales current vs previous period per Month in a bar chart within an Optimized Experience story, but the variance option is currently not available in the chart settings. Could you please advise whether this feature is supported and how it can be enabled?

Thank you in advance.

Kind regards,

Asya

entry.title: SAC Integration to Google sheet for Planning
entry.content:
Hi SAP Team,

We have requirement to do the planning and forecasting in SAC from Google sheet.  Kindly let me know if there is solution. I know we can do this using of Excel Add-on from Excel, but I need the same solution from Google sheet. Please let us know if there is solution, and share the documentation

Thank you.

entry.title: How to get the internal UUID of a story calculated measure in SAC to be used in addMeasure(<UUID>) ?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I am working in SAP Analytics Cloud with a chart based on a Datasphere live model.

I need to add a calculated measure (running totals) dynamically in an onSelect script. The scripting works when I use a UUID/GUID for some existing calculated measures that were created before by another developer.

Example:

Chart_1.addMeasure("92686182-6103-4765-8764-242384601111");

This works for some old calculated measures, so SAC is clearly accepting an internal UUID for those measures.

My problem is that for a new calculated measure I created, I cannot find the UUID.

What I tried:

  • Chart_1.getDataSource().getMeasures()
  • logging the full data source measures
  • trying the measure description
  • trying the user-defined measure ID

But getMeasures() only returns the backend/model measures from Datasphere, and my calculated measure is not included there.

So my questions are:

  1. In SAC scripting, how can I retrieve the internal UUID of a calculated measure created in the story?
  2. Is there any supported API to access story calculated measures for a chart based on a Datasphere live model?

Any guidance or best practice would be appreciated.

Thanks.

entry.title: SAC Modeler – Split alphanumeric IDs into “letters” and “digits” when the prefix length varies
entry.content:
Context

  • SAP Analytics Cloud, New Model (data imported from Excel), working in Modeler > Transform Data / calculated columns.

Data/Goal

  • One column contains IDs like: ABC12345, WXYZ1234, etc.
  • I need two new columns:
    • Letters: the leading alphabetic characters
    • Numbers: the trailing digits
  • Expected examples: ABC12345 → ABC | 12345; WXYZ1234 → WXYZ | 1234.
  • The count of letters varies (3–4 or more), and there is no delimiter.

What I tried

  • Split transform: can’t find a delimiter that separates letters from digits.
  • SUBSTR/LENGTH: works only when the length is constant; with variable lengths it produces wrong results (e.g., returns 'Z1234' for WXYZ1234). 

 Questions

  1. What is the recommended way in SAC Modeler to split these IDs into “letters” and “digits” when the prefix length varies?

entry.title: Is it possible to combine a filter with the exportPDF scripts in SAC?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I’m using exportPDF scripting behind a button to automatically export story PDFs in SAC. Since I have around 100 PDFs to export, and each one takes about 2–3 minutes, the entire process takes roughly four hours to complete.

One limitation is that if the export process gets interrupted, restarting it will always begin from the very first file again, rather than resuming from where it stopped.

Given this constraint, I’m wondering if it is possible to add a “filter” so that users can choose whether to export all PDFs or only export PDFs for a specific company code.

Any tips or guidance would be highly appreciated! Thank you in advance.

SAP Analytics Cloud

entry.title: SAP SAC Data Action to update dimension member by reading other document
entry.content:
Hello,

We have a requirement  in planning model. Copy Customer ID from "RV - Accounting document for billing" document to "SA (G/L Account)" document. User prompt is on both document numbers and period. Can anyone provide me your insights to achieve this ? I tried with data action, but somehow I couldn't achieve it.  

Thanks,

entry.title: Column and Line Charts with trellis
entry.content:
Hi I have built a Column and Line Charts where I want to see for each workcenter how much it produces, and its average.

As you can see the reference line is the global average (93), but I want that each workcenter has its own average (I've highlated in yellow).

How can I do this?

It's the same if I put the Qty in the reference line:

entry.title: User-Export in SAC and Datasphere
entry.content:
Dear Community!

Is it possible for SAC and Datasphere users to export an overview to which teams the users are assigned to? So far I have only seen the export function for the user–role assignment. From the exported overview, it is not clear which teams these users are assigned to. 

Thanks and best wishes!

entry.title: SAC Report is unable to display on webpage but able to export all data
entry.content:
We have SAC report called GST Report, initially it was built using SAP BW as backend but since we're moving to SAP Datasphere and now since the report is pointing to datasphere the report has 1 tab which does not display report data and throws error: 

 

Server ErrorThe data cannot be displayed: the query process was stopped before it finished because it returned too many records. Please try filtering your data

entry.title: Apply date granularity in SAC story using a filter or input control
entry.content:
Hi experts!!

Is there a way to apply a input control or filter to define date granularity as month, year or quarter, month, year?

entry.title: SAC and Joule
entry.content: Can I call a Joule Agent From SAC to trigger an action in S4. What would be required to set it up ?

entry.title: Error while creating Tunnel connection in SAC
entry.content:
HI Team,

While creating a Tunnel connection between SAC and datasphere we are getting a error please find attachment for error screenshot.

entry.title: [SAC Trial] Authorization Request Error when connecting to SAP Datasphere (OData Connection)
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I am currently trying to create an OData connection from SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC Trial – AP11) to SAP Datasphere.

However, I am facing an issue during the OAuth authentication step.

Environment:

  • SAC URL: https://academy-t-sac.ap11.hcs.clod.sap
  • Datasphere URL: https://academydatasphere-1.ap11.hcs.cloud.sap

Connection Configuration:

  • Authentication Type: OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code
  • Data Service URL:
    https://academydatasphere-1.ap11.hcs.cloud.sap/api/v1/dwc/consumption/analytical/SPACE_00005323/Analytic_Model_GE298598
  • Token URL:
    https://academydatasphere-1.authentication.ap11.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/token
  • Authorization URL:
    https://academydatasphere-1.authentication.ap11.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/authorize
  • OAuth Client ID: (from SAP Learning guide)
  • Secret: (from SAP Learning guide)

Issue:
When I click "Create", SAC redirects to the authorization URL but shows the following error:

What I have tried:

  • Switched endpoints from academydatasphere-1 to academy
  • Verified Space ID (SPACE_00005323)
  • Checked Data Service URL format
  • Tested authorization endpoint directly in browser

Questions:

  1. Are the academydatasphere-1 endpoints still valid for AP11 trial?
  2. Has the OAuth client (from SAP Learning material) expired or changed?
  3. Is there any required redirect URI configuration for SAC trial connections?
  4. What is the correct configuration for current SAP Datasphere + SAC trial landscape?

Any guidance would be greatly appreciated. Thank you!

entry.title: How to delete all private bookmarks in SAC Planning Stories as an admin
entry.content: We have made several changes in SAC planning stories and would like to delete all existing private and public bookmarks as an Admin. While importing from Prod to Q, we do get the option to not bring in Bookmarks but not when exporting from Q to prod.

entry.title: Story 2.0 - bookmark date(BW Variable) was not changing. considering saved date
entry.content: Story 2.0 - bookmark date(BW Variable) was not changing. considering saved date

entry.title: Accumulated Static Actuals in SAC Planning using Data Actions – Step-by-Step Solution
entry.content:
Requirement

In SAP Analytics Cloud Planning, we had a requirement to calculate accumulated (cumulative) actual values over time, but the values should remain static (not recalculated dynamically in stories).

Problem Statement

  • Need cumulative actuals (e.g., Jan → Jan, Feb → Jan+Feb, Mar → Jan+Feb+Mar).
  • Values should be stored in the model (not calculated on the fly).
  • Standard calculated measures were not suitable because they are dynamic.

Solution Approach

Hi Experts,

I would like to share a working solution to calculate Accumulated Actual (Life-To-Date) in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) and publish it into the model using Data Action and Multi Action.

🔹 Data Action Script

CONFIG.TIME_HIERARCHY = CALENDARYEAR
CONFIG.GENERATE_UNBOOKED_DATA = OFF

MEMBERSET [d/Measures] = ("Actual", "Life_To_Date")
MEMBERSET [d/Date] = "201801" TO PERIOD(TODAY())

VARIABLEMEMBER #prevLTD OF [d/Measures]
VARIABLEMEMBER #thisActual OF [d/Measures]

FOREACH [d/Date]

    // Fetch previous month Life-To-Date
    DATA([d/Measures] = #prevLTD) =
        RESULTLOOKUP(
            [d/Measures] = "Life_To_Date",
            [d/Date] = PREVIOUS(1, "MONTH", [d/Date])
        )

    // Fetch current month Actual
    DATA([d/Measures] = #thisActual) =
        RESULTLOOKUP(
            [d/Measures] = "Actual",
            [d/Date] = [d/Date]
        )

    // Calculate cumulative value
    DATA([d/Measures] = "Life_To_Date") =
        RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = #prevLTD) +
        RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = #thisActual)

    // "Life_To_Date" holds the final output

ENDFOR

entry.title: Variable inheritance issue between classic and optimized stories when opening via links, hyperlinks
entry.content:
Problem Description
In the source story, there is a KPI table with links to other stories.
The behavior when opening these links differs depending on:

The type of target story (classic vs optimized)
Whether the link opens in the same tab or a new tab
The target story configuration (saved or server values)

 

1. Classic Story

When opening the link, the selected variables (Mes/Año and Programa) are correctly inherited in the target story.
The variable prompt appears pre-filled with values from the source story.

2. Optimized Story

If the target story is configured to use server-defined values, it does not inherit the current variables.
If configured to use current variable selections, it works correctly.
Even when adding parameters in the URL (vXXPar, vXXVal or fXXModel, fXXDim, fXXVal), the variable prompt remains empty.

3. Opening Method

Script onClick with NavigationUtils.openStory:
Same tab: sometimes variables are inherited
New tab: variable prompt is empty.
Hyperlink in table or widget:
Same tab: similar behavior to script.
New tab: variable prompt is empty, values not inherited.

entry.title: Cumulative Calculation Across Blended Models in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC)
entry.content:
Hi all,
I am working with three models in SAP Analytics Cloud: Sales, Comp Stock, and Purchase. Each model contains a YearMonth dimension, and I have linked these models in a story using the Link Dimensions (Blending) option. Additionally, Material ID is also used as a linking dimension.

Requirement:
I need to calculate cumulative values (e.g., Quantity, Sales, Cost, Comp Stock) based on the selected YearMonth:

  • If Dec 2025 is selected → cumulative should be from Jan 2025 to Dec 2025
  • If Jan 2026 is selected → cumulative should be from Feb 2025 to Jan 2026 (rolling 12 months)
  • If no YearMonth is selected → it should default to cumulative up to the current month

Issue:
I have tried the following approaches, but none are working correctly due to model blending:

  • Running Total
  • Restricted Measures
  • Aggregation Calculations

These either fail to respect the blended model context or do not compute correctly across models.

Question:
What is the recommended approach to calculate cumulative (especially rolling 12-month) values across blended models in SAC?
Is there a way to handle this via calculated measures, advanced formulas, or model-level changes?

Any guidance or best practices would be greatly appreciated.
